name: Switch POP
description: The Switch POP lets you choose one of the inputs, and outputs the input unchanged. The number of points, vertices and primitives of all of the inputs can be different. It uses a numeric parameter, Index, to choose the input, with 0 referring to the first input. If Index is fractional - a non-integer, it can blend two inputs: If the inputs have the same number of points, vertices and primitives, they can be blended, two adjacent inputs at a time. Tip: Since it blends only adjacent inputs (input N with input N+1 or N-1), to blend from the last input to the first input, attach the first input again as the last input, and use CHOPs to loop from 0 to number-of-inputs snap back to 0 when Index reaches number-of-inputs. If Index is out of range, it clamps. See the Blend POP to blend arbitrary combinations of inputs. The Switch POP's architecture for handling multiple inputs is similar to the [[Attribute Combine POP]].

parameters.name: Extend
parameters.label: Extend
parameters.description: Determines what to do when the index exceeds the number of inputs. Allows for negative indices.

parameters.name: Blend between Inputs
parameters.label: Blend between Inputs
parameters.description: Blends the input POPs attributes when using floating point numbers in the index parameter.

parameters.name: Point Attribute Scope
parameters.label: Point Attribute Scope
parameters.description: The Point attribute scope to blend.

parameters.name: Primitive Attribute Scope
parameters.label: Primitive Attribute Scope
parameters.description: The Primitive attribute scope to blend.

parameters.name: Vertex Attribute Scope
parameters.label: Vertex Attribute Scope
parameters.description: The Vertex attribute scope to blend.

parameters.name: Input
parameters.label: Input
parameters.description: Start of Sequential Parameter Blocks managing the inputs of the POP.
